content: We can reframe the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework to incorporate multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research, creating a hierarchical self-refinement system that retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervision. This hybrid framework lets an LLM first generate multiple conflicting critiques of its own initial output from different problem-solving perspectives, then iteratively refine its output against these self-generated inconsistent perspectives rather than a single self-feedback signal.
